title: derris mollis
intro: Derris mollis, also known as the Kanthari or Poison Nut, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant's roots contain rotenone, a toxic compound with medicinal properties, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Derris mollis include its ability to act as an insecticide, a pain reliever, and an anti-inflammatory agent, providing health benefits such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating joint pain. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
